summary: HYPOTHESIS FALSIFIED. 60-day hold does NOT improve E1. Sharpe drops: 20d=0.658 → 60d=0.300 → 90d=0.251. Avg R improves (0.282→0.355→0.371) but trade count halves and win rate collapses (38.8%→26.7%→25.6%). Best pair at 60d: USDJPY (+0.478 Sharpe), worst: USDCAD (+0.125). NOTE: This standalone rate-sign-consistent backtest shows positive R at 20d (vs original state_allocator -0.186R) — difference is signal definition: 5-day consistency filter removes noise. The 5-day filter alone may be worth testing in state_allocator.

HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED AS REJECTED. March JPY seasonal: Pre-2020 (BOJ passive, n=6): 67% WR but avg R barely negative (-0.058). Post-2020 (BOJ/Fed active, n=5): 20% WR, avg R +1.948 against thesis (USD strengthened massively). 2022 worst year: USDJPY +6% in March during Fed hike panic. April shows no reversal (45% rose, +0.57% avg). Note: data only available 2014-2024, not 2010-2024. Key insight: the seasonal exists weakly pre-2020 but was never statistically robust. The 2022 Fed hike cycle permanently overwhelmed it. Not tradeable as standalone.

Pure carry (rate-sign, 3xATR stop) mostly negative Sharpe. Only GBPUSD (+0.178) and AUDUSD (+0.174) positive. USDJPY (-0.555) and EURUSD (-5.636) worst — large adverse moves vs high differentials. Current best carry: GBPJPY (447bps) and USDJPY (441bps) are actually the WORST performers due to BOJ vol. AUDNZD differential is 0bps right now — carry irrelevant. Key finding: pure carry-as-direction does not work. v004 works because it layers multiple signals. Carry income contributes but price momentum must align. Recommended pairs for carry overlay: GBPUSD and AUDUSD only.

CRITICAL features identified. Library has 63 patterns: 26 CRITICAL (severity>=2), 20 Elevated, 12 Bull, 5 Neutral. SHALE_SUPPLY_OIL_CRASH (primary match) features: momentum_decay dominant (-0.551), gold_weak (-0.429), ABOVE 200SMA (+0.427) — this is actually bullish equity price action during an oil crash. The match is commodity/macro-driven not equity-dislocation. CRITICAL fingerprint across all 26 CRITICAL patterns: consecutive_down_weeks (signal=2.22), sector_dispersion (1.99), realized_vol_90d (1.69), realized_vol_30d (1.46), realized_vol_10d (1.46). Implication: SHALE match is triggering on OIL-era macro conditions, not equity dislocation. The bull regime floor fix (0.50x) was correct. Full get_feature_weights() method recommended for AlexandrianLibrary.
